content:
- completed story-20 task 01 end-to-end: task file marked done/passes true, task switch executed, commit 94f03c6 created with the runner image contract and typed webhook routing changes, and final required gates already passed before commit
- the first git push raced the commit and returned too early, so push is being rerun after the commit to publish 94f03c6 cleanly
- should do next: none after the second push succeeds; quit immediately

- refreshed Ralph task summaries, read chooser rules, and confirmed there are no active unblocked failing bug tasks in `.ralph/current_tasks.md`
- inspected active task files for `blocked_by` and priority tags, checked the Rust workspace/codebase shape, and reviewed the just-finished story-20 runner-scratch-image work to choose the next task based on actual architecture rather than list order
- chose `.ralph/tasks/story-20-runner-scratch-image/02-task-enforce-the-runner-postgresql-only-runtime-contract.md` because it is unblocked, directly follows the newly completed story-20 task 01, and hardens the runner/verify/source boundary before workflow publication and novice-user verification depend on that split

- read prior progress log and confirmed the next active task is `.ralph/tasks/story-20-runner-scratch-image/02-task-enforce-the-runner-postgresql-only-runtime-contract.md`
- loaded the required `tdd` and `improve-code-boundaries` skill instructions before touching task execution
- reading the task file now to decide whether this turn must create a plan (`TO BE VERIFIED`) or execute an approved plan (`NOW EXECUTE`)

- read the active task file and confirmed it has no linked plan or execution marker, so this turn must be planning-only per the Ralph instructions
- next step is to inspect existing `.ralph/tasks/**/_plans/` conventions so the new plan file and task-file link match repo expectations
- after creating and linking the plan, quit immediately as required by the task protocol

- verified the task-plan linking format and started pulling neighboring runner/runtime-removal plans to avoid inventing a mismatched contract
- initial direct reads used guessed filenames and failed; next step is to list the actual plan filenames and read those instead
- also listed the current `crates/runner` files to ground the plan in the real module and test layout

- inspected adjacent runner and story-16 plans plus current runner runtime/config/tests to shape the new task-02 plan around real remaining gaps
- confirmed the runtime currently opens outbound connections only through `sqlx::postgres` and `PgConnection`, but the PostgreSQL-only/network-target contract is still implicit rather than owned by one explicit boundary and test suite
- next step is to create the task-02 plan file, link it from the task markdown, mark it `NOW EXECUTE`, and quit immediately per the Ralph workflow

- created and linked the new plan for `.ralph/tasks/story-20-runner-scratch-image/02-task-enforce-the-runner-postgresql-only-runtime-contract.md`
- the new plan is `.ralph/tasks/story-20-runner-scratch-image/02-task-enforce-the-runner-postgresql-only-runtime-contract_plans/2026-04-19-runner-postgresql-only-runtime-contract-plan.md` and ends in `NOW EXECUTE`
- the plan is centered on making PostgreSQL destination access an explicit typed boundary, rejecting source/verify config drift, consolidating runner runtime contract ownership in shared tests, and executing via TDD slices
- should do next: on the next turn, read the plan, execute it slice-by-slice with RED/GREEN TDD, run `make check`, `make lint`, and `make test`, and only touch `make test-long` if the task later proves it is required

- read prior Ralph progress, confirmed active task is `.ralph/tasks/story-20-runner-scratch-image/02-task-enforce-the-runner-postgresql-only-runtime-contract.md`, and loaded the required `tdd` plus `improve-code-boundaries` skill instructions
- next step is to read the task file and any linked execution plan to determine whether this turn is execution or must revert to planning

- read the active task markdown and confirmed it links to `.ralph/tasks/story-20-runner-scratch-image/02-task-enforce-the-runner-postgresql-only-runtime-contract_plans/2026-04-19-runner-postgresql-only-runtime-contract-plan.md`
- now reading the linked plan, checking git status for unrelated local changes, and loading the boundary-smell notes most relevant to contract/config ownership

- the linked task plan ends with `NOW EXECUTE`, so this turn must implement rather than re-plan unless the design proves wrong mid-flight
- now reading runner config, runtime, and contract-test files to find the first RED slice and the current generic destination boundary that should be flattened into an explicit PostgreSQL target

- execution design chosen: remove the generic `destination.connection` wrapper in favor of one explicit PostgreSQL target boundary owned directly by runner config/runtime types
- next step is to add RED contract tests first for the shared runtime contract helper and config drift, then change the config/runtime code and fixtures to match

- added the first RED default-lane contract for explicit runner network surfaces, made it pass via a shared `RunnerPublicContract` static audit, then started the main boundary refactor
- removed the `destination.connection` YAML wrapper across runner fixtures/examples and rewired core config/runtime types toward a single explicit PostgreSQL target boundary
- next step is to finish the remaining compile fixes from the type rename/refactor, rerun focused runner tests, then add explicit regression coverage rejecting the old wrapper and source/verify drift
